title: AI forces retailers, brands to rethink their product pages
gist: AI bot traffic to retail sites grew more than 5x from 2024 to 2025, pushing retailers and brands to redesign product pages for readability by AI agents like ChatGPT and Claude — including stripping JavaScript, adding text-only versions, and restructuring content so bots can surface it to shoppers.

title: Rising fuel costs push brands to adjust shipping policies
gist: Brands are raising free-shipping thresholds, adding delivery fees, and testing paid shipping as fuel costs rise following disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz, though a U.S.-Iran interim agreement may bring some relief.

title: Aritzia is achieving double-digit e-commerce growth in new markets, amid sped-up US store expansion
gist: Aritzia's new U.S. store openings in cities like St. Louis and Fort Worth are generating high double-digit lifts in digital sales in those local markets, with its chief digital officer describing physical stores as increasingly the first brand touchpoint that then drives online purchases.

title: WPP forecasts AI search ads to become fastest growing channel in advertising
gist: WPP forecasts AI search advertising — currently 1.9% of search ad revenue — will rise to nearly 40% of search revenue by 2031, making it the fastest-growing ad channel, driven partly by ChatGPT's ad rollout.

title: Why more better-for-you brands are leading with taste over health claims
gist: Better-for-you snack brands like Feel Good Foods and Good Pops are rebranding away from health-focused packaging toward taste and joy-driven messaging to reach mainstream shoppers beyond the core wellness audience.

title: Brands Briefing: Anthropologie’s tabletop games play
gist: Anthropologie is expanding its tabletop games assortment — up 34% in SKU count year over year — as it sees a 19% rise in board game page views, responding to consumer demand for tactile, offline experiences.

title: Takeaways on Google’s New AI Ad Features
gist: Google's Marketing Live announcements introduced AI-generated ad formats for AI Mode, an agentic 'Ask Advisor' campaign tool, and expanded Demand Gen capabilities including Merchant Center feed integration with creator-led video campaigns.

title: ‘Chic. Affordable. Divine.’ Isaac Mizrahi returns to Target
gist: Target has appointed Isaac Mizrahi as creative director at large, tasking him with advising on product concepts, design innovation, trend identification, and partnerships as the retailer works to recapture its 'Design for All' brand momentum.

title: Ad Tech Briefing: The shifting tides behind Publicis and The Trade Desk’s détente
gist: Publicis Groupe has reinstated The Trade Desk to its recommended DSP list following a March falling-out over alleged fee-stacking irregularities, with both parties issuing a brief joint statement focused on 'measurable outcomes for advertisers.'

title: ChatGPT’s market share slips below 50% for first time
gist: ChatGPT's global market share among AI assistants has fallen below 50% for the first time, dropping to 46.4% by May 2026 as Gemini (27.7%) and Claude (10.3%) grow; the report also notes ChatGPT is increasingly sending retail referral traffic to Target, Walmart, and Costco, while Amazon's crawler-blocking strategy has left it with stagnant AI-referred traffic.

title: Threads adds new personalization and community features as it reaches 500M monthly users
gist: Threads has reached 500 million monthly active users and is rolling out a private feed-control tool called 'Your Algo,' expanded Communities features, and Live Chats as it competes more directly with X.

title: Sixty percent of US consumers say ‘AI’ in brand messaging is a turnoff, survey finds
gist: A WordPress VIP survey of 2,000 respondents found 60% of US consumers are turned off by brands using 'AI' in their messaging, and 86% don't fully trust AI-generated answers — yet AI referrals to brand sites grew for 60% of enterprise respondents over the past year.

title: ‘BarkBox is not a box’: CEO
gist: Bark CEO Matt Meeker admitted the company 'fought the wrong battles' by over-optimizing a subscription personalization model that has become table stakes, as full-year revenue fell 18.5% to $394.8 million and the company pivots toward wholesale, marketplace, and lifestyle experiences.

title: Fashion is now the largest category on Whatnot as brands flock to the platform
gist: Whatnot, valued at over $11 billion after a $225M raise, has seen fashion overtake collectibles as its largest category, with 12 million fashion orders per month and 20 million new buyers in the past year. The platform ranked as the second-most-downloaded shopping app on the Apple App Store in April, above Amazon and eBay.

title: WTF is agentic commerce? | How brands can make loyalty and promotions agent-ready
gist: A sponsored guide from Talon.One outlines how AI agents are beginning to handle end-to-end shopping journeys, with Bain projecting agentic AI could represent 15–25% of total U.S. e-commerce by 2030. The piece argues brands need to make their loyalty and promotions infrastructure agent-readable to remain competitive.

title: 260 Sample Sale sees foot traffic jump as luxury customers seek value
gist: 260 Sample Sale, which runs off-price events for luxury and premium brands like Balmain and Dr. Barbara Sturm, reported a 10% foot traffic increase from 2024 to 2025 and is expanding into home and beauty categories. The growth reflects broad consumer demand for discounted premium goods across income levels.

title: OpenAI moves to automate ad creative
gist: OpenAI is expanding its ad pilot to include AI-powered creative tools that let advertisers automatically generate, modify, optimize, and localize ad creatives, going beyond just ad delivery support. The capability is outlined in an updated Ad Tools Terms document.

title: ‘We believe in America’: Nike global vp of soccer Camilo Andrade on the brand’s World Cup strategy
gist: Nike is centering its World Cup marketing around its 'Sport Offense' strategy under new CEO Elliott Hill, with a campaign featuring soccer legends and celebrities, plus product collaborations with names like Jacquemus. The push reflects a deliberate return to sports and teamwear as the brand's commercial core.

title: An Interview with Michael Morton About E-Commerce in the Age of AI
gist: Stratechery published a paywalled interview with Michael Morton covering e-commerce and AI, touching on distribution vs. referral models, grocery, and autonomous vehicles. Full content requires a paid subscription.

title: Faire opens its marketplace up to hotels, offices and non-retail customers
gist: Faire has officially expanded its wholesale marketplace beyond retail resellers to include hotels, offices, and other businesses buying products for their own operational use, after a pilot that drew roughly 5,000 non-resale buyers.

title: Etsy goes after Prime Day with ‘Shop Other Jeffs’ campaign
gist: Etsy launched a 'Shop Other Jeffs' campaign timed to Amazon's Prime Day (June 23–26), spotlighting individual sellers on the platform as a direct counter-positioning to Amazon's scale and anonymity.

title: New Ecommerce Tools: June 17, 2026
gist: A weekly roundup of new e-commerce tools covering AI personalization, agentic commerce infrastructure, bot protection, conversational search, LTL freight from Amazon, and payment integrations from Visa/OpenAI and Shopware/PayPal.

title: FTC lawsuit reveals how subscription scam networks evade app store enforcement
gist: The FTC sued Genesis Tech, alleging it ran a network of shell companies across Cyprus and Ukraine that generated nearly $700M through scammy auto-renewing subscription apps while evading Apple and Google enforcement mechanisms.

title: Marketplace Briefing: Shopify launches tools to help merchants track sales and traffic from AI platforms
gist: Shopify is adding a dashboard that lets merchants track orders and conversions coming from AI shopping channels like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Copilot, and see what questions shoppers ask those tools — plus gaps in product data that hurt visibility.

title: StockX to debut live shopping
gist: StockX is launching StockX Live this summer, bringing live auction formats — timed bidding, sudden death, pre-bidding — plus live chat to its marketplace, while simultaneously reopening a permanent retail location in NYC's SoHo.

title: Abercrombie & Fitch Co. brings Hollister to Target, expanding US wholesale operation
gist: Abercrombie & Fitch Co. is launching a multi-season wholesale partnership with Target, placing Hollister-branded dorm and home goods in most Target stores starting June 28, as part of a broader strategy to grow via wholesale and licensing after years of focusing that effort abroad.

title: Is Saks safe for brands again?
gist: Saks Global received court approval for its post-bankruptcy restructuring, emerging as a smaller operation with under 50 stores — mostly Neiman Marcus locations — and having fully exited the off-price segment.

title: I told them forced consent was unlawful. 5 years later it cost Elkjop €1.8M
gist: Nordic electronics retailer Elkjop was fined €1.8M by Norway's DPA after a consumer privacy expert's 2021 complaint showed the company's loyalty club required accepting marketing emails as a condition of membership — a textbook case of invalid, forced consent under GDPR.

title: Stitch Fix taps Walmart vet as chief product and technology officer
gist: Stitch Fix has hired Sree Sreedhararaj, formerly CTO at Ipsy and a Walmart Labs veteran, as its new chief product and technology officer to lead technology, product, data science, and IT as the company pursues an AI-driven turnaround strategy.

title: As AI scrutiny grows, DUDE Wipes points to supply chain savings and productivity gains
gist: DUDE Wipes says it has embedded AI tools across supply chain and operations, providing staff with Claude accounts and regular training, and is restructuring hiring to reduce agency dependency — citing concrete man-hour savings as evidence of ROI.

title: Pinterest introduces experimental AI app
gist: Pinterest launched Ask Pinterest, a limited-access experimental app that handles conversational, multi-step shopping queries using the platform's taste graph and visual data, with the stated goal of testing next-generation AI-powered shopping experiences.

title: 5 things to watch for this Prime Day
gist: Amazon's Prime Day runs June 23–26 this year — moved up from July, making it a four-day event and the unofficial start of summer sales season — against a backdrop of rising gas prices, inflation, but resilient consumer spending.

title: AI is now doing parts of merchants’ jobs, managing products and vendors
gist: Retailers are deploying AI agents to automate merchandising tasks including inventory management and vendor negotiations, with startups like Duvo.ai, Relex Solutions, and Gain leading early adoption in the U.S.

title: Polite Society is leveraging Ulta Beauty’s new TikTok Shop for its largest-ever affiliate campaign
gist: Makeup brand Polite Society is seeding its hero lip product to over 1,000 TikTok Shop affiliate creators as part of Ulta Beauty's new TikTok Shop launch, using the retailer partnership to drive awareness without celebrity backing.

title: Knix embarks on its biggest wholesale expansion, opening in 350 Target stores
gist: DTC intimates brand Knix, which has surpassed $1B in direct sales, is launching a children's sub-brand KT by Knix in over 350 Target stores — its first nationwide wholesale deal, amid 90% YoY wholesale growth.

title: The AI search boom is fueling content budgets before media buys
gist: Most marketers are directing AI search budgets toward content, creators, and tools to earn organic visibility in LLM-generated answers rather than paying for AI search ads, even as WPP forecasts AI search will become the fastest-growing ad channel.

title: Build an AI Flywheel for Ecommerce
gist: A McKinsey report on European e-commerce argues that AI delivers the most value not through isolated pilots but through interconnected 'flywheels' where personalization, demand signals, inventory, and pricing decisions reinforce each other — and Practical Ecommerce outlines how smaller merchants can start with a simple customer-feedback loop.

title: Walmart gets bullish on advertising business with Vibe.co acquisition
gist: Walmart is acquiring connected TV ad platform Vibe.co (reported at ~$1.4B) to expand Walmart Connect's full-funnel advertising capabilities, following its earlier Vizio acquisition. Walmart's global ad business grew 50% YoY in Q1, with advertising now representing roughly a third of operating income.

title: Walmart buys ‘the Google Ads of streaming’ Vibe in a deal tipped at a $1 billion-plus valuation
gist: Walmart has agreed to acquire Vibe.co, a CTV ad platform focused on SME advertisers, in a deal reportedly valued around $1 billion, extending Walmart's push to build a full advertising business through Walmart Connect.

title: Target launches back-to-school season — and the first test of its turnaround
gist: The retailer is debuting limited-edition collabs, savings on its loyalty program and a refreshed assortment as it competes with Amazon's Prime Day on summertime deals.

title: Talent agencies are training creators to act like retailers
gist: Talent agencies are increasingly coaching creators to operate like retailers — setting up storefronts, preparing for sales events, and demonstrating audience buying intent to brands, driven by social commerce and affiliate revenue streams. One mid-tier TikTok creator with 150K followers is generating $300K–$600K GMV monthly on TikTok Shop.

title: TikTok Shop’s Deals For You Days is a growth opportunity for early movers ahead of peak shopping season
gist: TikTok Shop's Deals For You Days summer event saw a 262% YoY increase in participating sellers in 2025 and a 1.6x sales uplift, positioning it as a meaningful pre-peak-season commerce moment for brands wanting to establish momentum before the crowded late-summer period.

title: Amazon’s latest ad format offers a glimpse of advertising’s agentic future
gist: Amazon announced 'Alexa+ Agentic Ads' at Cannes Lions, an ad format where users can complete a purchase entirely within a conversational AI interaction, without clicking through to a landing page. The format is in beta with partners like Papa Johns and The Orchard, with metrics and pricing still being worked out.

title: Exclusive: Claire’s turns its stores into a creator-commerce platform for Gen Alpha with Lana’s Life launch
gist: Claire's is launching a 45-piece collection with Roblox and YouTube creator Lana Rae, rolling it out to 866 stores across the US and Canada alongside VidCon fan experiences and Roblox rewards — updating its creator-to-retail playbook for Gen Alpha. The model builds on Claire's JoJo Siwa bow collaboration, which reportedly generated over $400M in retail sales.

title: How brands, retailers are using AI — and what they won’t let it touch
gist: At the CommerceNext Growth Summit, retail executives shared where they are deploying AI — back-office tasks like email management — and where they are drawing the line, particularly around in-store customer interactions. The conversation reflects a maturing view of AI as a tool requiring human oversight in high-touch areas.

title: Brands Briefing: How brands aim to stand out this summer as it’s become ‘harder to create momentum’
gist: Despite retail sales rising 0.9% in May, brand and agency executives say it has become harder to generate consumer momentum and cut through, even as macro spending holds up. The gap between aggregate spending data and individual brand difficulty is a recurring theme in current market conversations.

title: DoorDash launches conversational shopping assistant
gist: DoorDash has launched 'Ask DoorDash,' a conversational shopping assistant that builds grocery or restaurant carts from natural language descriptions, recipe photos, or shopping list images, relying on real-time merchant inventory data to generate relevant results. It is currently available in select US markets on iOS.

title: Gap Inc. plots AI-led marketing modernization effort
gist: Gap Inc. announced a multi-partner AI marketing modernization effort — combining Google Cloud for data unification, Zeta Global for AI-powered owned-channel personalization, and Publicis Sapient for marketing-to-behavior connectivity — with a stated goal of real-time, personalized customer experiences at scale. The announcement comes after nine consecutive quarters of positive comparable sales.

title: Poshmark opens its 120 million product listings to the Shazam of fashion
gist: Poshmark has integrated its 120 million active listings into Silvr, a visual search app that lets users photograph or screenshot outfits to find matches across retail and resale inventory. Poshmark sees the partnership as a new customer acquisition channel that intercepts shoppers before they enter a deliberate shopping session.

title: Cannes Briefing: The AI search crisis everyone’s talking about at Cannes is actually about brand coherence
gist: The dominant behind-the-scenes conversation at Cannes Lions 2025 is not about creative but about how brands get discovered and recommended by AI systems that aggregate signals from SEO, PR, content, and product indiscriminately. The core problem identified is cross-functional coordination: an LLM's answer about a brand is only as coherent as the totality of signals it has been trained on.

title: Marketplace Briefing: Prime Day starts softly as consumers buy gift cards, stock up on essentials, hold out for deeper discounts
gist: Prime Day 2025 opened with cautious consumer spending: shoppers are prioritizing essentials and holding back on big-ticket items, waiting to see if discounts deepen.

title: Allbirds officially changes name, appoints new CEO
gist: Allbirds has rebranded as Smartbird Inc. and is pivoting entirely to AI infrastructure (GPUaaS), after selling its footwear brand and assets to American Exchange Group and WSG Brands; the company never turned a profit and saw net sales fall 20% in 2025.

title: How retailers are thinking about omnichannel in the age of AI
gist: Executives from Ulta, Stitch Fix, Boot Barn, and Tapestry described omnichannel retail as increasingly shaped by AI-driven discovery and loyalty programs, with Ulta noting 95% of its sales flow through its 47-million-member loyalty program.

title: Lowe’s makes it easier to order decorative geese, above-ground pools and other marketplace items in over 1,700 stores
gist: Lowe's integrated its third-party marketplace into the store-associate software used across 1,700+ locations, allowing employees to search and purchase marketplace items for customers in-store with home delivery.

title: Repositioning retail for the AI era
gist: A sponsored piece describes how Macy's is embedding AI into core retail operations — search, personalization, inventory, and software development — framing it as an 'AI-first' operating philosophy rather than a feature layer.

title: Top Barriers of Cross-Border Shoppers
gist: A DHL survey of 29,000 shoppers across 29 countries identifies the top reasons international buyers abandon U.S. e-commerce sites: unexpected duties (63%), missing payment methods (62%), high delivery costs (45%), unclear return policies, and lack of trust.

title: The Backroom: The weight-loss era is not just about selling clothes
gist: Growing use of GLP-1 weight-loss drugs is creating 'fit volatility' in apparel — shoppers are cycling through body changes and abandoning or restarting prescriptions, which is complicating merchandising, sizing, and inventory planning for fashion retailers.

title: Indian payments chief thinks AI will be heavily involved in next era of digital payment growth
gist: NPCI CEO Dilip Asbe sees AI as the driver for UPI's next growth phase — from reaching new users via voice and multilingual tools to fraud detection and credit access for merchants with digital footprints.

title: Instagram is testing more ways to customize ‘Your Algorithm’
gist: Instagram is testing new ways to surface its 'Your Algorithm' content-tuning feature more prominently, including pull-down menus in the feed and swipe-up prompts on Reels, with the stated goal of making it central to the user experience.

title: The best response to AI slop and online noise is from Robin Williams
gist: The piece uses Robin Williams's Good Will Hunting bench speech as a framework for arguing that lived human experience — not aggregated knowledge — is what gives creative and professional work meaning in an era of AI-generated content.

title: India Ecommerce Paths for Foreign Sellers
gist: India's e-commerce market is $125B but heavily regulated for foreign sellers, who must choose between owning a marketplace, partnering with local distributors, selling D2C with significant legal hurdles, or shipping cross-border with high import duties and slow delivery.

title: The loyalty metric no one wants to explain
gist: Most loyalty and rewards programs report repeat purchase rates without measuring incrementality — i.e., they cannot prove whether customers returned because of the incentive or would have returned anyway, making many reported 'loyalty' outcomes statistically meaningless.

title: Stocky is shutting down, but that’s not the real story
gist: Shopify is shutting down its Stocky inventory app in August, with the article (sponsored by Katana) arguing this reflects that most growing brands already sell across multiple channels and need multi-channel inventory management, not a single-store tool.

title: How 3 brands are using TikTok Shop to expand abroad
gist: Three brands — a gaming console maker, a K-beauty brand, and a luxury resale platform — describe using U.S. TikTok Shop momentum as a launchpad for international expansion into the U.K., Europe, and Japan, emphasizing localized creator partnerships and platform-native experimentation.

title: Streaming is the next frontier for Walmart’s, Kroger’s ads businesses
gist: Walmart agreed to acquire connected TV ad platform Vibe.co for over $1B, continuing its push to let advertisers use Walmart shopper data to prove that streaming TV ads drive in-store and online purchases.

title: Amazon’s Prime Day drives online sales in the US up 9.3%
gist: U.S. online retail spend grew 9.3% YoY to $26.4 billion during Amazon's Prime Day (June 23–26), with shoppers trading up to more expensive items and BNPL usage jumping 9.5%. The event is now approaching Black Friday-scale spend, with roughly 40% of tracked retailers running deeper promotions than last year.

title: What’s next for international shipping as EU ends de minimis exemption
gist: The EU is eliminating its €150 de minimis exemption on July 1, replacing it with a temporary flat €3 customs duty per declared line item on low-value packages. A five-SKU shipment under €150 will now incur a €15 fee rather than no duty at all.

title: Consumer spending rises as inflation speeds up to three-year high
gist: U.S. consumer spending rose 0.7% in May despite PCE inflation hitting 4.1% annually, the fastest pace in three years, while income rose at the same rate. Markets now price an 80% chance the Fed raises rates by year-end, with core inflation expected to stay above target through 2028.

title: Electronics prices are starting to soar, adding to the stress consumers are facing
gist: Apple, Microsoft, and Nintendo have all announced significant price increases on laptops, tablets, and game consoles, driven by a memory and storage chip shortage caused by AI data center demand. The MacBook Pro gained $300, Xbox consoles will rise $150–$200 in August, and Nintendo Switch 2 will increase $50 in September.

title: Amazon launches AI assistant for German sellers
gist: Amazon has launched its AI-powered Seller Assistant inside Seller Central in Germany, designed to help sellers find information and make business decisions more efficiently.

title: Search Console’s New AI Data Is a Start
gist: Google Search Console has added a 'Generative AI' section showing URL impressions in AI Overviews and AI Mode, but the report has no click-through data, no feature-specific filters, and counts impressions even when users never see the cited URL. Site owners can now also block their content from appearing in Google's AI answers.
